description: A Department of Labor designed slogan graphic combining a portrait of President Trump with a quoted news excerpt. The background is a desaturated black-and-white photograph of Donald Trump in a dark suit and tie (with a small American flag lapel pin) walking through what appears to be a White House colonnade, looking toward the camera. A large bold white serif headline reads 'AMERICANS FIRST!'. Below sits a white rounded rectangle text card headed by the 'Newsweek' logo containing the quote: 'The U.S. Department of Labor (DOL) has vowed to ensure employers enforce H-1B visa rules and "prioritize qualified Americans" over foreign workers in a crackdown on discriminatory hiring dubbed "Project Firewall." ... The government's renewed focus on preventing discrimination against American workers reverberates through key industries, potentially impacting technology, research and corporate hiring.' Propaganda technique: pairing a strong, authoritative leader portrait with a slogan and a cited mainstream-press passage to lend credibility to the H-1B/'Project Firewall' crackdown framed as protecting American workers from foreign labor. Target audience: American workers, nativist/'America First' supporters, and tech-sector employees.
notable_text: AMERICANS FIRST! / Project Firewall / prioritize qualified Americans over foreign workers
